EXH_0918.description_text:
«미술관 길목 프로젝트»는 청주관 주차장 입구부터 본관 로비까지의 유휴공간을 다양한 실험적 예술 작품을 소개하는 공간으로 활용하는 프로젝트이다. 동시에 청주관 관람을 목적으로 도착한 관람객을 로비로 자연스럽게 유도하여 혼란을 막고자 만든 일종의 ‘웰컴존welcome zone’이다.

이번 프로젝트에 첫 번째로 선정된 작가 정세인(1985~)은 건축 자재인 타공판을 사용한 입체적인 평면 작업에서 강렬한 색감과 감각적인 타이포그래피 텍스트를 사용하여 다층적인 의미를 가진 이미지를 만든다. 작가는 이번 «미술관 길목 프로젝트»에서 타이포 설치작업과 타이포를 활용한 6개의 영상작업이 이어져 메들리 형식으로 보여주는 작업을 준비했다. 우선 오랜 시간 다뤄온 텍스트 작업을 자신만의 언어로, 좀 더 확장된 시각 매체- 타이포그래피 설치 작업과 영상 미디어 작업으로 만들었다.

«미술관 길목 프로젝트 : 정세인»은 국립현대미술관 청주관이 갖고 있는 고유한 건축적 구조와 공간을 활용한 프로젝트다. 이번 2023년 «미술관 길목 프로젝트»의 작가로 선정된 정세인은 인간 내면의 풍경과 폭발하는 감정, 그리고 그 속에서 치유되고 자유를 얻는 과정을 언어와 이미지를 통해 보여준다. 청주관 외벽과 길목을 지나는 방문객은 «미술관 길목 프로젝트»에서 일종의 내면적인 정서를 마주하고 평소 무심했던 자신을 향한 메시지와 의미를 되짚어 보길 희망한다.
EXH_0918.description_text_en: The National Museum of Modern and Contemporary Art (MMCA , Director Kim Sunghee) plans to unveil the 'Welcome Zone Project: Jung Sane.' The project aims to captivate the initial attention of visitors arriving at the parking lot by showcasing artworks on the outer walls and pathways of the MMCA Cheongju Art Storage Center (hereafter referred to as the MMCA Cheongju). The project opens on October 31 (Tuesday) this year and will run until October 31 (Thursday), 2024. The Welcome Zone Project aims to make use of the idle space stretching from the entrance of the MMCA Cheongju parking lot to the main lobby, transforming it into an area showcasing various experimental artistic projects. This space will also act as a 'welcome zone,' designed to organically guide those intending to explore the museum towards the main lobby, thereby preventing potential confusion. The launch of the Welcome Zone Project by MMCA Cheongju this year aims to ensure that visitors coming to view exhibitions could be guided with accurate information via the artworks. This was especially crucial given that the parking area is intertwined with exits leading to several neighboring institutions. Central to its purpose is showcasing artists whose work has a characteristic of visibility and clarity, mirroring the unique nature of the space-a 'pathway' bridging the main building and the parking lot and serving as the route visitors take to enter and exit. The artist chosen for the project's inaugural edition, Jung Sane (1985~) , creates layered images using a vivid color palette and evocative typographic texts. She employs architectural materials, specifically perforated boards, in her three-dimensional planar artworks. For this project, Jung is set to present a series of typographic installations along with six typography-imbued video works, all arranged in a medley format. Jung will reinterpret the text-based works she has developed over the years in her distinctive style, broadening their scope into more encompassing visual mediums: typographic installations and videos. For this project, an 'L-shaped' space within the museum entrance pathway is curated as a media installation zone, featuring four screens. As part of the commission, the artist will be bringing forward and rejuvenating several textual creations she has crafted over her career, presenting them anew as media artworks. These artworks are prominently displayed in the media installation zone. Displayed in the media installation zone are six new media pieces titled "Art in Borders" (2023), "The Ball’s Ballet" (2023), "Move Medley" (2023), "Weighting and Bearing" (2023), "Don’t Stop" (2023), and "Question Can Answer" (2023). These works are intended to engage visitors whether they are passing through the pathway space or pausing to observe, and this particular way of exhibition embodies Jung's tacit intention for visitors to engage deeply with the visualized textual content, interpreting each of the six works at a pace that resonates with them. "I Feel My Power" (2023) is a prominent installation mounted on the rear exterior wall of MMCA Cheongju. In this work, there are two sentences inscribed onto perforated panels: one that reads as, “… [my] story … is a mixture of nonsense and chaos, madness and dreams,” a quote from Hermann Hesse's novel "Demian: Die Geschichte einer Jugend von Emil Sinclair" (1919), and the other which is a sentence from the prologue of the artist's 2012 work titled "I Draw A Gun To Kill Myself Today" which reads, “I drew the horizon today because I wanted to see the sea." The artist adopted the quote from Hesse’s Demian as a message to share her life and philosophy with the audience. In the same vein, the sentence following the quote is a deeply personal recollection of her early-day artistic trainings that led her depict the sea often. Layered upon these phrases is another statement, "Trust your own power and don't stop believing." This encapsulates the artist's core message, emphasizing the intrinsic strength (your own power) each individual possesses and the freedom that can be derived from it. The artist uses the concept of 'porosity,' a key element showcased in her previous works, as a central theme in the Welcome Zone Project, and this concept is highlighted in “I Feel My Power” (2023). The cold metallic material of the aluminum perforated panels used in the artwork seems reminiscent of the artist’s breathing holes, each appearing to exhale words. The obscured, smudged red text behind these perforations can be perceived as the artist’s internal identity, revealing itself like an illusion; the dramatic unfolding of these emotions is presented in Jung Sane's unique style. While text-based works typically convey meaning directly through visual perception, the interplay between English and Korean in Jung Sane's pieces renders the text into an almost illegible image. This immersion into porosity demands a more deliberate and focused effort in the act of reading, which is characteristic of the artist’s distinct identity in her practice. The Welcome Zone Project: Jung Sane is an initiative that harnesses the unique architectural structure and space of the National Museum of Modern and Contemporary Art Cheongju. As the artist selected for the inaugural Welcome Zone Project, Jung Sane vividly portrays the landscapes of the human psyche, the explosion of emotions, and the ensuing healing and freedom achieved, all through language and imagery. MMCA Cheongju hopes that visitors passing by the museum’s exterior and through its pathways will embark on an introspective emotional journey via the Welcome Zone Project, encouraging them to reconsider the often-overlooked messages and meanings directed at themselves.
